creator: martin@email.aon.at
text:
[Please note that the email address given is not valid due to
	security reasons. Just reply to the gnats entry, and I'll follow
	it via the web interface.]

	I upgraded ports to latest, including following the advice given
	in UPDATING (portupgrade -rf libxcb, fixing xorg.conf).

	With this new setup, the machine crashes (reboots instantaneously)
	whenever I try to exit the X server (via any means). This also
	means that I cannot shutdown the machine cleanly.

	Yesterday, I rebuilt the whole xorg ports chain (using
	portupgrade -fR xorg-7.4), but the behavior stays the same.

	I am including two Xorg.0.log files, one from the initial
	upgrading run, the other after yesterday's rebuild of xorg.
	The first one is from a run with an edited /etc/X11/xorg.conf,
	the second is without any xorg.conf at all. Both might be
	truncated due to the crash (although I synced before trying
	to shut down the server).

	I am also including the output of pciconf -lv of the machine
	in question.

	As a final note, ports/131016 seems to describe very similar
	problems, albeit without the hard crash I am experiencing.

	I'd be happy if this could be fixed soon, as this bug renders
	the machine pretty much useless. If you need any further
	information, please feel free to add to this bug report - I'll
	check back regularly.

-------------------- begin Xorg.0.log.old --------------------
